questions.question: Who is eligible to customize their Notion Sites?

options.explanation: The article explicitly states that this feature is only available on paid plans.
While Enterprise is a paid plan, the article specifies 'paid plans' generally, but the primary restriction is that it is not free.
Correct. The feature is restricted to users on paid plans.
The article refers to 'paid plans' broadly rather than limiting it specifically to Team plans.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a theme option available for your Notion Site?

options.explanation: System is listed as one of the valid theme options under the Theme section.
Light is listed as one of the valid theme options under the Theme section.
Pink is not a theme option. The available options are System, Light, and Dark.
Dark is listed as one of the valid theme options under the Theme section.

questions.question: How can you remove the Notion watermark from your Site's header?

options.text: Turn it off in the header settings
options.explanation: While you can toggle it on or off, you can only turn it off if you are using a custom domain.

options.text: Turn it off in the header settings only if using a custom domain
options.explanation: Correct. The article notes that the Notion watermark can only be turned off if your Site is using a custom domain.
Purchasing the add-on is required to use a custom domain, but you must also toggle the watermark off in the settings.

options.text: It cannot be removed from the header
options.explanation: The article states you can toggle it on or off, subject to the custom domain restriction.

questions.question: To change the image that appears when sharing your Notion Site link, where should you go?

options.explanation: The correct path is Share > Publish > Site customization.
Correct. Under Site customization, you will find the Share preview section where you can upload an image.
Changing the cover image only affects the internal page preview, not the share preview.
SEO settings control the title and description, not the share preview image.

questions.question: Which browser currently does not support custom favicons?

options.explanation: Chrome supports custom favicons.
Firefox supports custom favicons.
Correct. There is a specific note stating that custom favicons are not currently supported in the Safari browser.
Edge supports custom favicons.

questions.question: When connecting Google Analytics, what specific identifier must you enter?

options.explanation: While you might use this elsewhere, the Notion Help Center specifically asks for the 'measurement ID'.
Correct. You must enter your Google Analytics measurement ID in the Google Analytics section of Site customization.
There is no 'Site ID' field mentioned for Google Analytics integration in this article.
The article specifies that you must enter the measurement ID, not an API key.

questions.question: What elements can be customized for SEO purposes?

options.explanation: Correct. You can customize the page title and description, which are used for share previews and SEO.
The article lists title and description, not keywords specifically as a separate customization item.
The URL slug is likely managed elsewhere, but the article focuses on title and description for SEO.
The article mentions title and description are used for SEO, not specifically meta tags.

questions.question: How do you add a navigation link to help visitors navigate to another page?

options.explanation: Correct. Under the Header settings, you select Navigation and then 'Add page'.
The article specifies the Header, not the Footer.

options.text: Add a link in the Share settings
options.explanation: Links are added under the Header > Navigation section within Site customization.

options.text: Go to the page and drag a block
options.explanation: This describes how to edit content, not how to add navigation links.

questions.question: Where is the 'Publish Changes' button located within the Site customization interface?

options.text: At the bottom of the screen
options.explanation: The button is consistently located at the top of the screen in all customization sections.

options.text: At the top of the screen
options.explanation: Correct. Whether customizing the header, favicon, or theme, you select 'Publish changes' at the top of the screen.

options.text: On the right side of the preview window
options.explanation: The button is not located on the right side of the preview window.

options.text: It is not a button, you just save automatically
options.explanation: The article explicitly instructs you to select 'Publish changes' at the top of the screen.

questions.question: Which of the following can be toggled in the header settings?

options.explanation: Correct. You can toggle Breadcrumbs, Search, and the Notion watermark on or off.
The article mentions adding navigation links, but does not list a 'Logo and Branding' toggle.
Social media links are not mentioned as a toggleable option in the header section.
While Breadcrumbs is one option, Search and the watermark are also available to toggle.

questions.question: How do you purchase a custom domain to use with your Notion Site?

options.text: Buy the domain name directly from Notion
options.explanation: You do not buy the domain directly from Notion. You use your existing domain or purchase the add-on.
Correct. The article states you can purchase the custom domain add-on.
You do not need to contact support to purchase the add-on; it is a direct purchase action.
While Notion provides a default subdomain, purchasing the add-on is required for a fully custom domain.

questions.question: How can you set a favicon using your existing page icon?

options.text: Select 'Page icon' from the Favicon dropdown
options.explanation: Correct. Under the Favicon dropdown in Site customization, you can select 'Page icon' to use your existing icon.

options.text: Select 'Upload image' and then choose the icon file
options.explanation: While you can upload an image, the specific option to use the *existing* page icon is 'Page icon'.

options.text: The favicon must always be an uploaded image
options.explanation: The article states you can select 'Page icon' from the dropdown to use your existing icon.

options.text: You cannot use your page icon as a favicon
options.explanation: The article explicitly lists 'Page icon' as an option in the favicon dropdown.

questions.question: What is the path to access Site customization?

options.explanation: Correct. You must open Share at the top of the published page, then click Publish, and finally select Site customization.
The path is 'Share' at the top of the page, not 'Settings'.
The customization is accessed via the top-level 'Share' menu, not the header settings.
The article describes the process as opening Share on the published page, not via a dashboard.
